methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series D → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.5x) to $640M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $700M - $6.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

private.name: Redwood Materials
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series D), post-money $6B+

data.narrative:
Solugen, a Series D climate and energy technology company, has raised $640M+ to date. Valuation analysis relies on two applicable methods triangulated against a peer set of five same-sector, same/adjacent-stage companies: Redwood Materials ($6.0B, Series D), KoBold Metals ($2.0B, Series C), Watershed ($1.8B, Series C), Electric Hydrogen ($1.0B, Series C), and Amogy ($700M, Series C).

Stage-progression methodology applies a 1.5x step-up multiplier to the $640M total raised proxy, implying a $960M valuation. Peer-median analysis yields $1.8B, the median across the five-company cohort with a range of $700M to $6.0B. The dispersion reflects heterogeneity in technology maturity and commercialization timelines within the climate tech sector.

Triangulation across both methods supports a valuation range of $816M (low) to $2.1B (high), with a median of $1.4B. The gap between stage-progression ($960M) and peer-median ($1.8B) reflects uncertainty around Solugen's relative competitive positioning and capital efficiency within the peer set. Key sensitivities include technology differentiation, path to unit economics, and the timing of revenue inflection relative to comparable-stage peers.
